initial_candidates.sql:
WITH country_insertion_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) AS insertion_days_count
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
  GROUP BY country_code_2
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),
country_with_nine_days AS (
  SELECT country_code_2
  FROM country_insertion_counts
),
consecutive_periods AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    insert_date,
    DATE(insert_date, '-' || CAST(ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country_code_2 ORDER BY insert_date) AS TEXT) || ' days') AS grp
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
    AND country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
),
consecutive_stretches AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    grp,
    MIN(insert_date) AS start_date,
    MAX(insert_date) AS end_date,
    COUNT(*) AS consecutive_days
  FROM consecutive_periods
  GROUP BY country_code_2, grp
),
longest_consecutive_period AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    start_date,
    end_date
  FROM consecutive_stretches
  ORDER BY consecutive_days DESC, start_date ASC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
  CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN c.capital = 'Y' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS capital_proportion
FROM cities c
INNER JOIN longest_consecutive_period lcp 
  ON c.country_code_2 = lcp.country_code_2
  AND c.insert_date BETWEEN lcp.start_date AND lcp.end_date;

WITH country_insertion_days AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) AS insertion_days
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
  GROUP BY country_code_2
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),
country_with_nine_days AS (
  SELECT country_code_2
  FROM country_insertion_days
),
consecutive_periods AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    insert_date,
    DATE(insert_date, '-' || CAST(ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country_code_2 ORDER BY insert_date) AS TEXT) || ' days') AS grp
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
    AND country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
),
longest_consecutive_period AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    grp,
    MIN(insert_date) AS period_start,
    MAX(insert_date) AS period_end,
    COUNT(*) AS days_count
  FROM consecutive_periods
  GROUP BY country_code_2, grp
  ORDER BY days_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
),
capital_entries_in_period AS (
  SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_entries,
    SUM(CASE WHEN capital = 'Y' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS capital_entries
  FROM cities c
  JOIN longest_consecutive_period lcp 
    ON c.country_code_2 = lcp.country_code_2
   AND c.insert_date BETWEEN lcp.period_start AND lcp.period_end
)
SELECT 
  CAST(capital_entries AS REAL) / total_entries AS proportion_from_capital
FROM capital_entries_in_period;

WITH country_insertion_days AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) AS insertion_days_count
    FROM cities
    WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
    GROUP BY country_code_2
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),
country_with_nine_days AS (
    SELECT country_code_2
    FROM country_insertion_days
),
consecutive_periods AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        insert_date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country_code_2 ORDER BY insert_date) AS rn,
        DATE(insert_date, '-' || (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country_code_2 ORDER BY insert_date) - 1) || ' days') AS grp
    FROM cities
    WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
      AND country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
),
consecutive_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        grp,
        MIN(insert_date) AS period_start,
        MAX(insert_date) AS period_end,
        COUNT(*) AS consecutive_days
    FROM consecutive_periods
    GROUP BY country_code_2, grp
),
longest_consecutive_period AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        period_start,
        period_end
    FROM consecutive_groups
    WHERE consecutive_days = (SELECT MAX(consecutive_days) FROM consecutive_groups)
),
entries_in_longest_period AS (
    SELECT 
        c.country_code_2,
        c.capital,
        CASE WHEN c.capital = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_capital_entry
    FROM cities c
    JOIN longest_consecutive_period lcp 
        ON c.country_code_2 = lcp.country_code_2
       AND c.insert_date BETWEEN lcp.period_start AND lcp.period_end
)
SELECT 
    CAST(SUM(is_capital_entry) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS capital_proportion
FROM entries_in_longest_period;

WITH country_insertion_days AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) AS insertion_days_count
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
  GROUP BY country_code_2
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),
country_with_nine_days AS (
  SELECT country_code_2
  FROM country_insertion_days
),
consecutive_periods AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    insert_date,
    DATE(insert_date, '-' || CAST(ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country_code_2 ORDER BY insert_date) AS TEXT) || ' day') AS grp
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
    AND country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
),
consecutive_period_lengths AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    grp,
    COUNT(*) AS consecutive_days,
    MIN(insert_date) AS period_start,
    MAX(insert_date) AS period_end
  FROM consecutive_periods
  GROUP BY country_code_2, grp
),
longest_consecutive_period AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    period_start,
    period_end
  FROM consecutive_period_lengths
  WHERE consecutive_days = (SELECT MAX(consecutive_days) FROM consecutive_period_lengths)
),
capital_entries_in_longest_period AS (
  SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS capital_count
  FROM cities c
  JOIN longest_consecutive_period lcp 
    ON c.country_code_2 = lcp.country_code_2
    AND c.insert_date BETWEEN lcp.period_start AND lcp.period_end
  WHERE c.capital = 1
),
total_entries_in_longest_period AS (
  SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_count
  FROM cities c
  JOIN longest_consecutive_period lcp 
    ON c.country_code_2 = lcp.country_code_2
    AND c.insert_date BETWEEN lcp.period_start AND lcp.period_end
)
SELECT 
  CAST(capital_count AS REAL) / total_count AS proportion_from_capital
FROM capital_entries_in_longest_period, total_entries_in_longest_period;

WITH country_insertion_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) AS insertion_days
    FROM cities
    WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
    GROUP BY country_code_2
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),
country_with_nine_days AS (
    SELECT country_code_2
    FROM country_insertion_counts
),
consecutive_periods AS (
    SELECT 
        c.country_code_2,
        c.insert_date,
        DATE(c.insert_date, '-' || (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cities c2 
                                     WHERE c2.country_code_2 = c.country_code_2 
                                       AND c2.insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31' 
                                       AND c2.insert_date <= c.insert_date) || ' days') AS grp
    FROM cities c
    WHERE c.country_code_2 IN (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
      AND c.insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
),
consecutive_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        grp,
        COUNT(*) AS consecutive_days
    FROM consecutive_periods
    GROUP BY country_code_2, grp
),
longest_consecutive AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        MAX(consecutive_days) AS max_consecutive
    FROM consecutive_counts
    GROUP BY country_code_2
),
longest_period_details AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.country_code_2,
        cc.grp,
        MIN(c.insert_date) AS start_date,
        MAX(c.insert_date) AS end_date
    FROM consecutive_counts cc
    JOIN cities c ON c.country_code_2 = cc.country_code_2 
                  AND DATE(c.insert_date, '-' || (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cities c3 
                                                  WHERE c3.country_code_2 = c.country_code_2 
                                                    AND c3.insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31' 
                                                    AND c3.insert_date <= c.insert_date) || ' days') = cc.grp
    WHERE cc.country_code_2 IN (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
      AND cc.consecutive_days = (SELECT max_consecutive FROM longest_consecutive lc WHERE lc.country_code_2 = cc.country_code_2)
    GROUP BY cc.country_code_2, cc.grp
),
capital_entries AS (
    SELECT 
        c.country_code_2,
        COUNT(*) AS total_entries,
        SUM(CASE WHEN c.capital = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS capital_entries
    FROM cities c
    JOIN longest_period_details lpd ON c.country_code_2 = lpd.country_code_2
                                   AND c.insert_date BETWEEN lpd.start_date AND lpd.end_date
    WHERE c.country_code_2 IN (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
    GROUP BY c.country_code_2
)
SELECT 
    CAST(ce.capital_entries AS REAL) / ce.total_entries AS proportion
FROM capital_entries ce;

  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) AS insertion_days
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'

consecutive_periods AS (

    country_code_2,

  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'

longest_consecutive_period AS (

    SUM(CASE WHEN capital = 'Y' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS capital_entries
  FROM cities c
  JOIN longest_consecutive_period lcp

candidates.sql:
WITH country_insertion_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) AS insertion_days
    FROM cities
    WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
    GROUP BY country_code_2
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),
country_with_nine_days AS (
    SELECT country_code_2
    FROM country_insertion_counts
),
date_sequence AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        insert_date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country_code_2 ORDER BY insert_date) AS rn
    FROM cities
    WHERE country_code_2 IN (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
      AND insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
),
consecutive_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        insert_date,
        DATE(insert_date, '-' || (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM date_sequence ds2 
                                   WHERE ds2.country_code_2 = date_sequence.country_code_2 
                                     AND ds2.insert_date <= date_sequence.insert_date) || ' days') AS grp
    FROM date_sequence
),
consecutive_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        grp,
        COUNT(*) AS consecutive_days,
        MIN(insert_date) AS start_date,
        MAX(insert_date) AS end_date
    FROM consecutive_groups
    GROUP BY country_code_2, grp
),
longest_consecutive AS (
    SELECT 
        country_code_2,
        MAX(consecutive_days) AS max_consecutive
    FROM consecutive_counts
    GROUP BY country_code_2
),
longest_period AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.country_code_2,
        cc.start_date,
        cc.end_date
    FROM consecutive_counts cc
    JOIN longest_consecutive lc ON cc.country_code_2 = lc.country_code_2 
                               AND cc.consecutive_days = lc.max_consecutive
),
capital_entries AS (
    SELECT 
        c.country_code_2,
        COUNT(*) AS total_entries,
        SUM(CASE WHEN c.capital = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS capital_entries
    FROM cities c
    JOIN longest_period lp ON c.country_code_2 = lp.country_code_2
                          AND c.insert_date BETWEEN lp.start_date AND lp.end_date
    WHERE c.country_code_2 IN (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
    GROUP BY c.country_code_2
)
SELECT 
    CAST(ce.capital_entries AS REAL) / ce.total_entries AS proportion
FROM capital_entries ce;

WITH country_insertion_days AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) AS insertion_days
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
  GROUP BY country_code_2
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insert_date) = 9
),
country_with_nine_days AS (
  SELECT country_code_2
  FROM country_insertion_days
),
filtered_cities AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    insert_date,
    capital,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country_code_2 ORDER BY insert_date) AS rn
  FROM cities
  WHERE insert_date BETWEEN '2022-01-01' AND '2022-01-31'
    AND country_code_2 = (SELECT country_code_2 FROM country_with_nine_days)
),
grouped_periods AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    insert_date,
    capital,
    CAST(julianday(insert_date) - julianday('2022-01-01') - rn AS INTEGER) AS grp
  FROM filtered_cities
),
period_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    country_code_2,
    grp,
    MIN(insert_date) AS period_start,
    MAX(insert_date) AS period_end,
    COUNT(*) AS days_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN capital = 'Y' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS capital_count
  FROM grouped_periods
  GROUP BY country_code_2, grp
  ORDER BY days_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
  CAST(capital_count AS REAL) / days_count AS proportion_from_capital
FROM period_stats;
